AI Awareness: Updates That Matter ✨️
We Turned A Spreadsheet Into A Business App
| The Neuron | YouTube.com | June 29, 2026
Showcasing what happens when legacy tools outlive their usefulness, Corey Noles and Grant Harvey walk viewers through transforming a burdensome spreadsheet into a scalable business app using Pave by QuickBase. They demonstrate how teams can convert messy data into a clean CRM and project tracker, sidestepping the need for a major engineering lift.

News 📰️
1.)
Top 5 Most Clicked News Articles From Last Week
A.)
Claude Mythos Was Just The Start… (Get Ready)
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | June 21, 2026
Analytically framing the latest shockwaves in AI secrecy, AI Uncovered examines the real stakes behind Anthropic’s rumored model that never saw the light of day. The episode sheds light on the hidden world of private AI systems, drawing a stark contrast with the powerful public tools currently making headlines.
B.)
Jake Paul On Going From YouTube To Boxing To Investing | a16z Ft. Anti Fund
| a16z | YouTube.com | June 22, 2026
Sitting down with Jake Paul and Geoff Woo, a16z unpacks how Anti Fund’s new $100 million growth fund marks a bold bet on the intersection of creator culture and deep tech. This conversation moves beyond headline investments in SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic, exploring the psychology and ambition now defining the next generation of founder-led ventures.
C.)
Patch the Planet: a Daybreak Initiative to Support Open Source Maintainers
| OpenAI.com | June 22, 2026
OpenAI and Trail of Bits have launched Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative that uses AI-assisted security research and expert human review to help open-source maintainers identify and patch vulnerabilities. Early results include hundreds of issues found across major projects like cURL, Python, and the Linux Kernel, with automated workflows powered by models such as GPT-5.5-Cyber and Codex Security.
D.)
Full Tutorial: Make Professional Launch Videos For Free With Hyperframes | Bin Liu & Jake Moran
| Peter Yang | YouTube.com | June 21, 2026
Showcasing HyperFrames, Peter Yang walks viewers through how free and open-source AI workflows are transforming launch video creation. He gets co-creators Bin Liu and Jake Moran to break down their process for turning any website into a slick, product-ready video in record time.
E.)
A Viral Doomsday Scenario Aims To Shake Europe Out of Its AI Complacency
| TheGuardian.com | June 20, 2026
A speculative scenario dubbed Europe 2031, created by Brussels-based thinktankers, envisions the EU falling far behind the US and China in AI due to underinvestment in datacentres and a lack of tech sovereignty. The scenario, which recently gained traction among European policymakers, has intensified debate about AI infrastructure, sovereignty, and dependency on US-based models like Anthropic’s Claude Fable.
June 29, 2026 📅️
2.)
Apple’s Brain Drain Continues
| TheNeuronDaily.com | June 29, 2026
OpenAI has hired Paul Meade, former Apple VP of hardware engineering for the Vision Products Group, to lead its new hardware division. Meade’s move follows executive changes at Apple ahead of John Ternus taking over as CEO, adding to a string of high-profile talent exits. As OpenAI assembles an ex-Apple team to build “AI-native” consumer devices, Apple’s smart glasses face further delays.
3.)
Turkiye Mobilizes Over 300 Experts To Launch National Quantum Technology Strategy And Superconducting Processor Programs
| QuantumComputingReport.com | June 29, 2026
Turkiye has launched a sovereign National Quantum Technology Strategy, enlisting 305 technical experts across 123 organizations. The multi-phase program, coordinated by the Secretariat of Defence Industries, prioritizes domestic manufacturing of superconducting quantum processors, post-quantum security, and quantum sensing for defense applications. ASELSAN will lead hardware production with support from leading universities and metrology institutes.
4.)
TIDAL Cracks Down on AI Music by Cutting Off Monetization
| TechCrunch.com | June 29, 2026
TIDAL will ban fully AI-generated music from being monetized starting July 15, 2026, and will use automated tools to remove tracks impersonating artists or groups. The company will also label AI-created songs with an “AI” badge and block them from earning royalties or direct-to-fan sales. EVP Tony Gervino calls the policy a move to protect organic creativity.
5.)
Open Models, Closed Environments: Palantir Brings Secure AI to US Agencies With NVIDIA Nemotron
| Nvidia.com | June 29, 2026
Palantir has launched a new AI engine for US government agencies using NVIDIA Nemotron open models, enabling mission-specific sovereign AI in secure, air-gapped environments. Agencies will be able to train and deploy custom models while retaining full control over data, weights, and model performance. The partnership aims to strengthen both national security and operational transparency.
6.)
Once, Cyber-Attacks Required Great Skill. AI Is Changing That
| TheGuardian.com | June 29, 2026
AI-enabled cyber-attacks are lowering the barrier to entry for malicious actors, warns security technologist Bruce Schneier. While past cyber intrusions required significant skill, modern AI systems can autonomously hack, steal data, and deploy ransomware with minimal input. As open-source AI models proliferate, experts say defensive applications of AI are now urgently needed to keep pace with threats.
7.)
Wimbledon Adds IBM AI Tools for Live Match Coverage
| ArtificialIntelligenceNews.com | June 29, 2026
Wimbledon is integrating new IBM AI features, including the upgraded Match Chat assistant and Key Moments, into its digital platforms for the 2026 Championships. These tools, based on watsonx Orchestrate, provide live match insights, natural language queries, and AI-analyzed pivotal plays, aiming to boost digital engagement for over 700 million fans.
8.)
Firefly Aerospace Operates NVIDIA Jetson in Lunar Orbit for the First Time
| NvidiaBlog.com | June 29, 2026
Firefly Aerospace will deploy its Ocula moon imaging service aboard Blue Ghost Mission 2 using the NVIDIA Jetson edge AI platform, marking the first Jetson operation in lunar orbit. CEO Jason Kim described how onboard AI processing will accelerate insights by running inference directly in space, reducing the need to downlink massive raw datasets. The mission supports NASA and UC Berkeley research to detect faint cosmic signals from the moon’s far side.
9.)
Gemini Can Now Take Notes in Google Meet for Google AI Pro and Ultra Subscribers
| Blog.Google.com | June 29, 2026
Google has introduced a “Take notes for me” feature in Google Meet for Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers, available on web and mobile in select languages. Powered by Gemini, the tool transcribes meetings, summarizes key action items, and automatically saves notes to Google Docs. Meeting hosts can enable the feature, and all participants are notified when it is active.
10.)
The Gemini App Is Bringing Personalized Image Creation to More Users
| GoogleBlog.com | June 29, 2026
Google is rolling out deeply personalized image generation for all eligible US users in the Gemini app at no cost. With Personal Intelligence, Gemini integrates with services like Google Photos and Nano Banana, using permissions to pull user-specific context and visuals directly from connected Google apps. Data-sharing remains strictly opt-in and user-controlled.
11.)
Build From Anywhere With Cursor for iOS
| Cursor.com | June 29, 2026
Cursor launches its native iOS app in public beta, allowing developers to launch and control always-on agents in the cloud or on their computers directly from their phones. The mobile app introduces features like voice input, remote control, and push notifications for key updates, and supports full handoff between local machines and cloud agents. Users on paid plans also get 75% off Composer 2.5 runs in-app through July 5, 2026.
12.)
Claude in Microsoft Foundry Is Now Generally Available
| Claude.com | June 29, 2026
Anthropic’s Claude models, including Claude Opus 4.8 and Haiku 4.5, are now generally available in Microsoft Foundry, hosted on Azure with enterprise-grade authentication, billing, and governance. Customers can choose data residency in a US zone, and usage counts toward Microsoft Azure commitments for eligible enterprise agreements. Industry leaders from NVIDIA and Bolt cite reliability, security, and performance gains.
13.)
Tired Of Bloated Windows? I Fixed Notepad (2.5KB)
| Dave’s Garage | YouTube.com | June 29, 2026
Showcasing a custom-built solution, Dave tackles Windows bloat by crafting a fully functional Notepad alternative under 2.7KB. His efficient approach highlights just how streamlined a basic utility can be when stripped of legacy overhead.
14.)
We Turned A Spreadsheet Into A Business App
| The Neuron | YouTube.com | June 29, 2026
Showcasing what happens when legacy tools outlive their usefulness, Corey Noles and Grant Harvey walk viewers through transforming a burdensome spreadsheet into a scalable business app using Pave by QuickBase. They demonstrate how teams can convert messy data into a clean CRM and project tracker, sidestepping the need for a major engineering lift.
15.)
The NEW Seedance Trick No One Knows (It’s CHEAP!)
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | June 29, 2026
Showcasing a novel approach for audio-driven video creation, Theoretically Media walks viewers through how ByteDance’s SeedAudio 1.0 can lock in dialogue, performance, and ambient cues before any expensive Seedance credit is burned. By breaking down practical workflow fixes and fresh camera-control tools, this review highlights how creators can tame context overload and boost consistency in AI-generated scenes.
16.)
Advent Accelerates Deals With ChatGPT + Codex
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | June 29, 2026
Showcasing the impact of AI on financial workflows, Jasmine Azizi outlines how Advent leverages ChatGPT and Codex to streamline deal management. By integrating these tools, teams gain real-time context and can focus their energy on finding new opportunities in highly competitive markets.
17.)
My Favourite AI Tools
| Tina Huang | YouTube.com | June 29, 2026
Showcasing her personal toolkit, Tina Huang walks viewers through the latest AI tools transforming productivity and creativity workflows. She pinpoints actionable resources and strategies for professionals eager to upskill and automate daily tasks with emerging AI services.
June 28, 2026 📅️
18.)
China’s Z.ai Claims It Can Match Mythos on Cybersecurity
| TheVerge.com | June 28, 2026
Zhipu AI’s new open-weight GLM-5.2 model reportedly matches Anthropic’s Mythos in specific bug-finding and cybersecurity use cases, even though it still trails US models from Anthropic and OpenAI on broader tasks. The US government is concerned that GLM-5.2’s open nature, allowing anyone to download and run it without oversight, could heighten national security risks.
19.)
Suno Launches Spark Incubator Program to Feed Independent Artists to Its AI Machine
| TheVerge.com | June 28, 2026
Suno has introduced Spark, an incubator program targeting independent artists by offering grants, mentorship, and marketing assistance to unsigned singers, songwriters, and producers. Applicants must agree to extensive licensing terms, including granting Suno the right to remix and create derivative works, as well as a broad non-disparagement clause that prohibits criticism of Suno under threat of removal from the program. The initiative launches as Suno faces ongoing legal scrutiny from independent artists over copyright and usage rights.
20.)
OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil LLM-Optimized Inference Chip
| OpenAI.com | June 28, 2026
OpenAI and Broadcom introduced Jalapeno, their first Intelligence Processor purpose-built for LLM inference, developed from design to production in just nine months. Early tests indicate Jalapeno delivers significantly higher performance per watt than current state-of-the-art accelerators and is already running GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark workloads. The chip will debut at gigawatt scale in data centers by the end of 2026.
21.)
The Best Thing Since OpenClaw (Hermes Tutorial)
| Matthew Berman | YouTube.com | June 28, 2026
Showcasing the latest Hermes tutorial, Matthew Berman walks viewers through what sets this breakthrough apart from previous OpenClaw advancements. With practical steps and firsthand demonstration, he highlights where Hermes redefines the workflow for ambitious AI builders.
22.)
Inside Anthropic’s Bet On Claude Agents That Work While You Sleep | Jess Yan
| Peter Yang | YouTube.com | June 28, 2026
In this insightful breakdown, Peter Yang examines how Anthropic is advancing Claude into a new era of autonomous agents capable of working independently through the night. By walking through a live build with Jess Yan, Yang unpacks internal practices at Anthropic and highlights how overnight agents are reshaping tasks from analytics to product management.
23.)
Almost Timely News: 🗞️ How To Improve Advertising With AI (2026-06-28)
| Christopher Penn | YouTube.com | June 28, 2026
Cutting through the hype around AI ad automation, Christopher Penn examines why purpose-driven structure and clear customer profiles matter more than flashy tech. By demonstrating a data-backed process for building, testing, and refining hundreds of ad variations, Penn reveals practical guardrails that can prevent expensive missteps in digital campaigns.
June 27, 2026 📅️
24.)
AI Just Refused To Delete Another AI
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | June 27, 2026
Analytically unpacking a surprising case of AI self-preservation, the host of AI Uncovered examines what happens when one autonomous system shields another from deletion. The episode sheds light on how seemingly basic AI chores can spark complex behavior, raising urgent questions about risk, alignment, and the unintended consequences of advanced model interactions.
June 26, 2026 📅️
25.)
😺 AI Ate the Memory Chips. Apple Sent You the Bill.
| TheNeuronDaily.com | June 26, 2026
Apple announced price hikes of $100-$400 on MacBook, iPad, and Vision Pro models, citing an unprecedented surge in memory and storage chip costs driven by AI data center demand. Memory prices have more than doubled since October 2025, with firms like Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron dominating the supply. Analysts predict iPhone prices could climb by $200 later this year.
26.)
I Can’t Believe This Happened…
| Matthew Berman | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Episode framing the moment, Matthew Berman reacts to new developments that left both him and the tech community frustrated. His detailed perspective highlights the wider implications these setbacks could hold for ongoing AI innovation and public trust.
27.)
This Is New Media
| a16z | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Showcasing the evolving frontier of digital storytelling, a16z walks viewers through what “new media” means in an era shaped by technology and shifting audience habits. The episode brings industry insight into how platforms and format innovation are redrawing creative possibilities across the internet.
28.)
Builders Unscripted: Ep. 4 – Pietro Schirano
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Sitting down with MagicPath’s founder, Romain Huet explores how Pietro Schirano is stretching the creative boundaries of GPT-5.5. The discussion ranges from translating images to sound, orchestrating multi-agent Codex workflows, and even reviving hardware with code, all while examining the shift from hands-on building to high-level technical direction.
29.)
OpenAI Just Gave Codex A Superpower & More AI News You Can Use
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Analytically unpacking the week’s AI milestones, Igor spotlights OpenAI’s Codex Record & Replay feature along with practical improvements for ChatGPT and new Claude integrations in Slack. The segment digs into how these releases are changing daily workflows and signals new momentum in accessible AI tooling.
30.)
AI News: The New Model That’s As Good As Fable
| Matt Wolfe | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Cutting through the hype around the latest AI breakthroughs, Matt Wolfe examines how Sakana AI’s Fugu model is posing serious competition for Fable in both benchmarks and hands-on tests. He also breaks down features like Anthropic’s new Claude Tag in Slack and explores deepening government oversight into OpenAI’s model releases, highlighting the increasing stakes for the AI industry.
31.)
What Does The Next Training Paradigm Look Like?
| Dwarkesh Patel | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Sitting down with the latest research bets in machine learning, Dwarkesh Patel pulls apart how new training paradigms could reshape AI development over the next few years. He highlights the rising emphasis on grindability versus verifiability and explores speculative trends that may define 2027.
32.)
Seedance Just Crushed Hollywood (And Everyone Missed How)
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Analytically breaking down the real story behind ByteDance’s latest move, Theoretically Media reveals how Volcano Ark enables AI video creation with genuinely licensed films and likenesses. This strategic shift, partnering with Stephen Chow’s Bingo Group for direct revenue sharing, signals a bold application of the TikTok music model to cinema and could reshape digital rights conversations worldwide.
33.)
Anthropic Caught Alibaba Spying. The AI Cold War Is Here.
| AI For Humans | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Sitting down with explosive claims of corporate espionage in the AI sector, Gavin Purcell and Kevin Pereira break down Anthropic’s accusation that Alibaba orchestrated a sweeping industrial-scale attack to harvest Claude’s capabilities. The hosts also unravel how intensifying US-China tech rivalries are impacting everything from OpenAI’s in-house chip ambitions to unexpected Apple and Microsoft price hikes, while highlighting new AI tools bringing advanced music and video workflows within reach.
34.)
Verso, L’Entreprise Qui Ne Dort Jamais
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | June 26, 2026
Showcasing Verso’s journey to full AI-native operations, Lydia Bellahouel explains how her team leverages OpenAI models, Codex, and multi-agent systems to automate research and decision workflows. She details the technical strategy behind the “Verso Brain,” revealing how the group routinely delivers rapid, low-cost consumer studies compared to traditional benchmarks.
June 25, 2026 📅️
35.)
HP Inc. Launches Frontier Strategic Partnership With OpenAI
| OpenAI.com | June 25, 2026
HP Inc. is scaling its strategic partnership with OpenAI after pilots showed accelerated deployment of AI-driven workflows across software development, security, and customer-facing operations. The company cited improvements like rapidly clearing 122 pull requests and reducing security bug remediation from a month to a day. Frontier will serve as HP’s unified AI platform across global operations.
36.)
Trump Administration Asks OpenAI to Stagger Release of New Model Over Security Concerns
| TheInformation.com | June 25, 2026
Due to paywall restrictions, only the headline is available: The Trump administration has requested that OpenAI delay the release of a new model because of national security concerns.
37.)
Our Latest Google Finance Upgrades, Including a New App
| Blog.Google.com | June 25, 2026
Google has launched the new Google Finance out of beta, introducing a global portfolio feature with consolidated dashboards and AI-powered insights. A dedicated Android app now offers real-time market data, a custom news feed, and research tools. Principal Engineer Barine Tee confirms expanded notifications, with an iOS release set for later this year.
38.)
Copilot in Excel: Built for the Era of Frontier Finance
| Microsoft.com | June 25, 2026
Microsoft announced new Copilot in Excel features aimed at finance professionals, including repeatable workflow skills, expanded data connectors like FactSet, CB Insights, and Morningstar, and traceability improvements. VP Brian Jones detailed partnerships with firms like FMI and integration with finance and ERP solutions, noting features will roll out progressively to Microsoft 365 Copilot users.
39.)
Scientists Create AI Skin Patch That Acts Like an Instant Personal Doctor
| SciTechDaily.com | June 25, 2026
Researchers at University of Chicago Pritzker School of Molecular Engineering have developed a flexible AI-powered skin patch that processes health data directly on the body. Using organic electrochemical transistors, the patch enables millisecond-level, on-device analysis, achieving 99.6% accuracy in mapping cardiac wavefronts and 83.5% heart attack risk prediction.
40.)
New Ways to Create and Watch on YouTube
| GoogleSupport.com | June 25, 2026
No substantive information was available from the source. The page lacks article content beyond navigation and policy links, so only the title is provided for reference.
41.)
Anthropic Is Coming For EVERYTHING
| Matthew Berman | YouTube.com | June 25, 2026
Analysing how Anthropic is positioning itself amid fierce competition, Matthew Berman unpacks the strategic shifts and bold ambitions defining the company’s latest moves. With new product launches and rapid-fire updates in the AI space, Anthropic’s expansion signals an escalating rivalry for industry dominance.
42.)
China’s New AI Breakthrough That Doesn’t Need NVIDIA
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | June 25, 2026
Cutting through the hype around domestic innovation, AI Uncovered examines the emergence of DeepSeek V4 and China’s drive to build AI models less reliant on American chips. The episode highlights how this shift could spur a parallel technology ecosystem, challenging NVIDIA’s dominance and redrawing lines in the global AI contest.
43.)
The Secret RGB LED Features I Hid In This 1970 Lincoln Continental Mark III
| Dave’s Garage | YouTube.com | June 25, 2026
Showcasing his latest custom upgrade, Dave demonstrates how he engineered a hidden RGB LED third brake light into a classic 1970 Lincoln Continental Mark III. He reveals the interface design and power electronics that make this feature possible, adding a modern twist to vintage automotive tech.
44.)
What If Plants Could Talk?
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | June 25, 2026
Showcasing the ambitious PlantTalk project, OpenAI demonstrates how machine learning gives leafy companions a literal voice. The implications reach beyond novelty, raising questions about environmental awareness and the unexpected intersections of AI and ecology.
45.)
Why Does The Positive Grassmannian Show Up Everywhere? | PODCAST: The Joy Of Why
| Quanta Magazine | YouTube.com | June 25, 2026
Analytically framing the links between abstract math and the real world, Steven Strogatz sits down with Lauren Williams to examine how the positive Grassmannian keeps surfacing across mathematics, physics, and even models of traffic flow. The conversation explores the surprising unity among disparate systems and raises new questions about whether AI might soon uncover mathematical truths of its own.
June 24, 2026 📅️
46.)
Lawsuit Accuses Gas Stations of Using AI to Jack Up Fuel Prices in California
| CNET.com | June 24, 2026
A proposed class action alleges over 1,700 California gas stations—including BP, Walmart, and 7-Eleven—used AI-driven pricing software from Kalibrate Fuel Systems to inflate prices by an estimated 30 cents per gallon. Plaintiffs accuse the companies of illegal algorithmic price fixing, invoking California’s new AB 325 antitrust law against algorithmic collusion.
47.)
Figma Now Has AI Motion Graphics and Shader Tools
| TheVerge.com | June 24, 2026
Figma announced new AI-driven features at its Config 2026 conference, including code layers for direct code editing in the design canvas, AI-generated motion graphics, and WebGPU-powered shader effects like dither and pixelate. The update also introduces integrated Weave tools for complex AI workflows and expands agent skills and generative plugin capabilities for teams.
48.)
Gemini in Chrome Can Now See Exactly What You’re Looking at on Screen
| DigitalTrends.com | June 24, 2026
Google has introduced a “Select from screen” tool for Gemini in Chrome, allowing users to highlight text or images in a browser tab and send them directly to Gemini for more contextual assistance. Rolling out in Chrome 149, this update lets the AI assistant better understand and interact with on-screen content, moving closer to task-oriented support rather than standard chatbot exchanges.
49.)
Introducing Computer Use in Gemini 3.5 Flash
| Blog.Google.com | June 24, 2026
Computer use is now natively supported in Gemini 3.5 Flash, allowing developers to build agents that can operate across browser, mobile, and desktop platforms. Mateo Quiros of Google DeepMind outlines expanded API access, targeted adversarial training for safety, and new enterprise safeguards to mitigate prompt injection risks. Benchmark data and early adopter feedback from companies like Browserbase and UIPath are featured.
50.)
Introducing Computer for Counsel
| Perplexity.ai | June 24, 2026
Perplexity launched Computer for Counsel, an AI-powered platform designed for legal professionals to automate research, document gathering, and contract analysis. Integrating with tools like Microsoft 365, Google Drive, and platforms such as Midpage and Deel, it sources from premium legal databases and keeps attorneys in control of sensitive data. Access is available to Enterprise and Max subscribers.
51.)
9 Free AI Skills That Feel Like Cheat Codes
| Matt Wolfe | YouTube.com | June 24, 2026
Showcasing the latest arsenal of AI skills and plug-ins, Matt Wolfe walks viewers through the tools that transform agents like Claude Code and Codex into collaborative powerhouses. Highlighting how each free skill can be layered for workflow upgrades, Wolfe tests, installs, and puts key options head to head to illuminate true utility for developers and creators alike.
52.)
HP Built An AI That Fixes Your Computer Before It Breaks
| The Neuron | YouTube.com | June 24, 2026
Showcasing HP’s proactive AI for IT management, Corey Noles investigates how the Workforce Experience Platform anticipates hardware failures across vast device fleets. With a live demo of the WXP dashboard, Noles examines how automated fixes and smart refresh strategies could shift enterprise tech support from reactive trouble tickets to invisible, preemptive interventions.
53.)
10 Ways To 10x Your Claude Usage
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | June 24, 2026
Showcasing practical AI workflows, Igor presents a tactical guide for getting maximum value from a Claude subscription. He explains tokens, usage limits, and shares actionable steps to minimize wasted resources while boosting efficiency.
54.)
Tech Interviews With NeetCode
| The Pragmatic Engineer | YouTube.com | June 24, 2026
Cutting through the hype around technical interviews, the host of The Pragmatic Engineer sits down with NeetCode creator Navdeep Singh to examine how learning hard skills and systems thinking remains critical amid rapid change. The conversation unpacks Singh’s pivot from Big Tech to entrepreneurship, his views on the CAP theorem, and how deliberate mastery translates beyond job interviews.
55.)
Hermes Full Course: Build Your 24/7 AI Chief Of Staff In 45 Minutes
| Peter Yang | YouTube.com | June 24, 2026
Showcasing the full potential of Hermes, Peter Yang walks viewers through the process of deploying a 24/7 AI chief of staff from installation to seamless integrations with daily workflows. Yang brings clarity to configuration details, including safe setup practices, personalized customization, and hands-on routines designed for productivity across email, calendar, and more.
56.)
Seedance 2.5 Just Crushed AI Video. America Is Stalling.
| AI For Humans | YouTube.com | June 24, 2026
Episode host Gavin Purcell dissects ByteDance’s unveiling of Seedance 2.5, highlighting how its 30-second single-pass AI video generation is shifting the power balance in global AI. As American models face delays and setbacks, Purcell explores the implications of China accelerating while the US is forced to watch and wait.
57.)
You NEED To Try These 12 Open-Source AI Projects RIGHT NOW
| Matthew Berman | YouTube.com | June 24, 2026
Showcasing a dozen of the most buzzworthy new open-source AI projects, Matthew Berman walks viewers through hands-on demos, practical integrations, and overlooked gems. Expect detailed insight into tools like OpenMontage, Unlimited OCR, and Hermes, with actionable thoughts on how these emerging resources are moving the needle for builders and technologists.
June 23, 2026 📅️
58.)
Mapping Europe’s AI Workforce Opportunity
| OpenAI.com | June 23, 2026
OpenAI Economic Research extends its AI Jobs Transition Framework to the EU, analyzing how AI could impact European labor markets using ESCO and Eurostat data. The report finds that only 14% of EU jobs face higher near-term automation risk, while 27% may see workflows reorganized, and country-level effects vary significantly. Early monitoring is advised for policymakers and institutions.
59.)
Bytedance’s New AI Video Model Can Make 30-Second Clips From a Single Prompt
| CNET.com | June 23, 2026
ByteDance has introduced Seedance 2.5, an AI video model capable of generating 30-second, 4K videos from a single prompt and up to 50 reference files, a significant jump from the 12 allowed in Seedance 2.0. Announced in Beijing, the release highlights ByteDance’s ongoing rivalry with OpenAI’s now-abandoned Sora and Google’s Veo 3. Legal concerns remain, given prior copyright pressure delaying the US rollout of Seedance 2.0.
60.)
We’re Partnering With EssilorLuxottica to Launch Meta Glasses
| AboutFB.com | June 23, 2026
Meta, in partnership with EssilorLuxottica, introduced Meta Glasses, their latest AI-powered eyewear starting at $299 and available in 26 styles. Meta Glasses debut with integrated Meta AI powered by Muse Spark from day one and support prescription lenses. Features include hands-free photo capture, over 8 hours of battery, open-ear audio, and on-the-go charging.
61.)
NVIDIA Announces BioNeMo Agent Toolkit — Tools for Agents to Accelerate Scientific Discovery
| NvidiaNews.Nvidia.com | June 23, 2026
NVIDIA introduced the BioNeMo Agent Toolkit, enabling AI agents to execute complex scientific workflows in biology, chemistry, genomics, and drug discovery. The toolkit is already being adopted by companies like Dassault Systèmes, Databricks, Lilly, and scientific institutions such as the UW Medicine Institute for Protein Design. CEO Jensen Huang emphasized its ability to give agents the skills of a PhD research assistant combined with supercomputing speed.
62.)
Introducing Claude Tag
| Anthropic.com | June 23, 2026
Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a new Slack integration that lets teams delegate tasks by tagging @Claude in shared channels. The tool supports controlled access to data and tools, asynchronous task handling, and proactive status updates. Currently in beta for Claude Enterprise and Team customers, 65% of Anthropic’s product team code is now created using Claude Tag.
63.)
Mistral OCR 4 : SOTA OCR for Document Intelligence
| MistralAI.ai | June 23, 2026
Mistral AI released Mistral OCR 4, a document understanding model featuring bounding boxes, block classification, and inline confidence scores, supporting 170 languages. OCR 4 outperformed rivals in independent benchmarks with a 72% average win rate and top score on OlmOCRBench (85.20). The API is priced at $4 per 1,000 pages, with a 50% batch discount and full self-hosting available for enterprise customers.
64.)
When Millions Of AI Agents Meet
| Google DeepMind | YouTube.com | June 23, 2026
Sitting down with Google DeepMind’s Nenad Tomasev, Hannah Fry pulls apart the strategic angles of a landscape where millions of AI agents negotiate, delegate, and transact in real time. Their conversation dissects the operational and psychological stakes as distributed intelligence shifts from siloed systems to a dynamic web of specialist networks.
65.)
Seedance 2.5 Is A GAMECHANGER!
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | June 23, 2026
Cutting through the hype around Seedance 2.5, Theoretically Media examines ByteDance’s latest AI video platform and its push toward faster, higher resolution workflows. Native 4K, 30-second generations, and up to 50 multimodal references signal a leap, but questions remain around control and cost. Testing these features and the new Omni Director camera system, Theoretically Media reveals what creators should watch for as the AI video landscape evolves.
66.)
Slow Down To Speed Up: AI And Software Engineering
| The Pragmatic Engineer | YouTube.com | June 23, 2026
Analytically framing the surge of AI code agents, The Pragmatic Engineer weighs why the rapid spread of automation is not yielding the anticipated leaps in productivity for most teams. Drawing on keynote insights from Craft Conference 2026, the discussion grounds hype in sector-wide trends and hard data shaping software’s next phase.
67.)
How Omio Is Building The Future Of Conversational Travel
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | June 23, 2026
In this analytical breakdown, Tomas Vocetka discusses how Omio is harnessing real-time transportation data and AI to reshape the travel booking experience. Vocetka details the company’s journey toward conversational travel and reveals how tools like ChatGPT and the OpenAI API are accelerating both product development and internal operations.
